Name: Login
Notes: Sets cookie easyread_auth=mock-session (Next mock). Mock server also validates users/password against SQLite.

Name: Logout
Notes: Clears cookie easyread_auth (Next mock). Mock server clears auth cookie name auth.

Name: Session
Notes: Returns whether auth cookie is valid (Next mock).

Name: CurrentUser
Notes: Returns User (displayName/avatar/email). 401 when not authenticated.

Name: ListDocuments
Notes: Mock server selects id,title,created,status from documents. Next wraps the backend calls.

Name: CreateDocument
Request (JSON / Shape): { query: string, files?: string[], language_style: 'plain'|'easyread', auto_generate_images: boolean, image_generation_options?: object }
Notes: Mock server accepts query (required for title) OR files; at least one of (query or files) must be present. Creates status='pending' and returns 202+docId.

Name: GetDocument
Notes: Returns document parsed into {id,title,created,status}.

Name: UpdateDocumentTitle
Notes: Updates documents.title and modified timestamp.

Name: DeleteDocument
Notes: Deletes the document row.

Name: Upload
Response (JSON / Shape): { id, originalName, mimeType, size, previewUrl, createdAt }
Notes: Upload is handled by mock server via multer; frontend uploads PDF/DOCX files.

Name: ListDocSections
Notes: Mock server returns section ids for a document ordered by sort_order then created.

Name: CreateSection
Notes: Creates an empty section with next sort_order.

Name: ReorderSections
Request (JSON / Shape): { target: string, after?: string|null, before?: string|null }
Notes: Reorders sections within a document (updates sort_order).

Name: GetSection
Response (JSON / Shape): { id, document_id, text, image, sort_order, created, modified }
Notes: Frontend later normalizes/derives image URL and candidates.

Name: UpdateSection
Notes: Partial update of section fields; modified timestamp updated.

Name: DeleteSection
Notes: Deletes the section row.

Name: GetSectionCandidates
Notes: Mock returns COALESCE(image_url,id). Frontend treats returned strings as image URLs (or falls back).

Name: GenerateSectionCandidates
Notes: Mock server inserts candidate rows; returns candidateUrls (section.image + placeholders).

Name: ListImages
Notes: Frontend `fetchImages` expects ImageDTO[] from backend.

Name: User
Request (JSON / Shape): { displayName: string, avatar: string, email: string }
Notes: Used by `/api/users/me`.

Name: LoginPayload
Notes: Used by `login(payload)` in `src/lib/api/users.ts`.

Name: Doc
Response (JSON / Shape): { id: string, title: string, created: Date, status: 'ready'|'pending' }
Notes: Frontend normalized from backend document DTO.

Name: CreateDocumentPayload
Request (JSON / Shape): { files?: string[], query: string, language_style:'plain'|'easyread', auto_generate_images:boolean, image_generation_options?: Record<string,unknown> }
Notes: `files` are upload IDs; `query` is an enquiry string.

Name: Section
Response (JSON / Shape): { doc_id: string, id: string, order: number, text: string, image: string, candidates: string[] }
Notes: Frontend derives `image` and `candidates` (strings treated as image URLs).

Name: Image
Response (JSON / Shape): { id: string, created: Date, url: string }
Notes: Frontend model; `ImageDTO` uses ISO string for created.

Name: ImageDTO
Response (JSON / Shape): { id: string, created: string (ISO), url: string }
Notes: Network/serialization shape.

Name: uploads
column8: Columns: id TEXT PK, original_name TEXT, mime_type TEXT, size INTEGER, preview_url TEXT, created_at TEXT.

Name: documents
column8: Columns: id TEXT PK, title TEXT, files_json TEXT, language_style TEXT, auto_generate_images INTEGER, image_generation_options_json TEXT, created TEXT, modified TEXT, status TEXT.

Name: sections
column8: Columns: id TEXT PK, document_id TEXT FK->documents(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, text TEXT, image TEXT, sort_order INTEGER, created TEXT, modified TEXT.

Name: section_candidates
column8: Columns: id TEXT PK, section_id TEXT FK->sections(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, prompt TEXT, options_json TEXT, created_at TEXT, image_url TEXT.

Name: users
column8: Columns: id TEXT PK, email TEXT UNIQUE, password TEXT, name TEXT, created_at TEXT.

Name: auth_sessions
column8: Columns: id TEXT PK, user_id TEXT FK->users(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, created_at TEXT, expires_at TEXT.
